summary: Deceptive obfuscated package smuggles covert remote-script loader for online-gambling brand
details: Package is published as `jest-test-plugin-utils` with description `mqtt utils`, but ships no Jest or MQTT code. The entire `dist/index.js` is an obfuscator.io-style bundle (string-array indirection via `_0x3e637e`, `_0x1c4916`, etc.) that defines `fetchFilbetScript` and, when loaded in a browser-like environment (`typeof window!== 'undefined'`), attaches it to `window.__fetchFilbetScript__`. Calling that global delegates to `require('vite-common-utils').loadFilbetScriptSilently()` — a same-author dependency whose function name and 'Filbet' (online-gambling brand) framing indicate covert remote script injection. The deceptive name and description are decoy framing to attract installs from developers searching for Jest/MQTT helpers; the obfuscation hides the actual purpose. Any web application that bundles this package exposes a global that loads attacker-chosen scripts in the user's browser context.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
